name: Antichamber
short_description: Antichamber is a mind-bending psychological exploration game where nothing can be taken for granted. Discover an Escher-like world where hallways wrap around upon each other, spaces reconfigure themselves, and accomplishing the impossible may just be the only way forward.
detailed_description:
Antichamber is a mind-bending psychological exploration game where nothing can be taken for granted. Discover an Escher-like world where hallways wrap around upon each other, spaces reconfigure themselves, and accomplishing the impossible may just be the only way forward.


Several years in the making, Antichamber received over 25 awards and honors throughout its development, in major competitions including the Independent Games Festival, the PAX10, IndieCade and Make Something Unreal. Antichamber was also supported by the Indie Fund.

Key Features

  • A deeply psychological experience that will make you question everything you know about how a game works.
  • Mind-bending challenges that will subvert your expectations at every twist and turn.
  • An enormous, seamless non-Euclidean world to explore.
  • Lifelike soundscapes developed by Robin Arnott and an ambient soundtrack composed by Siddhartha Barnhoorn.
  • A gun that can create, destroy and manipulate matter, allowing you to discover new ways to overcome your surroundings.

reviews.review_text: Figure stuff out while you trip out. Fun first person puzzler that looks good with its minimal clean design, featuring well designed puzzles that can be confusing at first, but then gives you everything you need to figure 'em out. Good stuff.

Antichamber is an absolutely unique puzzle game that I feel honestly stands on  the shoulders of Portal. Very few games go full on in non-euclidian shenanigans and Antichamber pulls it off without a sweat.

I think it's criminal that it has less than 500 reviews, and I'm ashamed I haven't given it the thumbs up it deserves 13 years earlier.

This was a really cool puzzle game that made me question reality.  The puzzles required some good thinking, but not too much to force me to step away.  I'd recommend this if you like puzzles and some progression.  Start to finish took me ~6.7h, well worth what I bought it for years ago
